---
title: "About"
subtitle: "Who maintains this, and how to get in touch."
toc: false
---

## What this platform is

The Livestock Marketing Alternatives Explorer is an open, continuously-updated explorer of weekly feeder-cattle price data for the four-corners region, sourced directly from USDA-AMS Market News and supporting public-domain feeds (BLS CPI). The site exists to keep these comparisons alive as new public data lands — rather than as a one-time snapshot — and to give producers and Extension peers interactive tools to work against their own year ranges, weight classes, and deflation choices. Cash-side numbers are public-domain federal data; basis figures are a derived statistic against CME GF settles which are not redistributed by this repository (see [data licensing](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/LICENSE-DATA.md) for the full posture). Every figure is reproducible from versioned code; every page has an **edit on GitHub** link.

## Related work

A Sall & Tronstad extension article analyzing the same dataset is in preparation. The article extends and updates an earlier 1994 Extension chapter on livestock marketing alternatives (Tronstad, 1994). The platform develops its own methodology and is not bound to any particular published comparison; design choices on this site are documented under [Methodology](methodology/index.qmd) and stand on their own merits.

## What's currently in development

The [USDA RMA Livestock Risk Protection (LRP) explorer](lrp.qmd) is now live — a filterable explorer for ~210k feeder-cattle LRP endorsements (2003-present), defaulting to Arizona with state and year-window filters. County-level drill-down is a planned v2 enhancement.

Currently in development: an **LRP-vs-cash backtest** comparing realized LRP outcomes to selling at the Clovis cash auction over matched windows; **methodology-style explainer pages** for marketing alternatives that are described in the research literature but not yet built into the platform; and **additional regional auction integrations** (Willcox AZ, Utah feeders) so producers outside the four-corners region have closer benchmarks.

## Who maintains it

Primary author and maintainer: **Ibrahima Sall**, postdoctoral researcher in agricultural economics. The platform is maintained independently of any single institution. See [CITATION.cff](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/CITATION.cff) for the current author list.

## How to engage

- **Found a number that looks off?** Open an [issue](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/issues/new/choose) — every page has an "Edit on GitHub" link to jump straight in.
- **Want to adapt the platform to another region or state?** The code is MIT-licensed. Fork it; the data pipelines are independent of any particular region.
- **Extension peer who wants to review or contribute a methodology page?** Reach out via a GitHub issue.

## Governance

Read [GOVERNANCE.md](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md) for the full decision-authority matrix, the Automation Over Features commitment, and the acceptable-use posture. In short: every feature ships with a scheduled refresh, a failure detector, a last-known-good fallback, a vintage stamp, a methodology page, and license-compliant source attribution — or it does not ship.

## Privacy

This site does **not** use any third-party analytics or tracking services — no Google Analytics, no Facebook pixel, no advertising networks, no browser fingerprinters. Visitors are not tracked, no cookies are set, and no consent banner is required because there is nothing to consent to. The maintainer has visibility only into repo-level traffic (clones, stars, referrers to the GitHub repository page) via GitHub's built-in repo Insights — not into who visits the deployed site or what they do here.

## Acknowledgments

This platform sits at the intersection of publicly-funded federal data (BLS, USDA-AMS, USDA NASS) and publicly-released Extension research. It exists because those data are in the public domain and because Extension's mission is to put trustworthy information in reach of rural producers.
